# KXBTCD Threshold Strategy Research — Session 58
# Generated by research agent a2c85c03732383d76 on 2026-03-12

## Key Finding: Lognormal N(d2) pricing is the correct model

Same-day KXBTCD = digital cash-or-nothing call option.
Fair probability: P(above K) = N(d2) where d2 = ln(S/K) / (sigma * sqrt(T))

## Sigma Source: Deribit DVOL (free, no auth)
- URL: https://www.deribit.com/api/v2/public/get_index_price?index_name=btc_usdv
- DVOL 57 = daily move ~3.0%. Convert: daily_sigma = DVOL/100/sqrt(365)

## Same-Day (Type 2) Parameters
- Min T to enter: 2 hours before settlement
- Min edge: 7-8%
- Min spot-strike distance: 1%
- Kelly fraction: 0.10-0.15
- Best window: 9am-11am EDT (4-8 hours before 5pm slot)
- Hybrid signal: lognormal dominant + 2% weight intraday drift adjustment

## Friday Weekly (Type 3) Parameters
- Min T to enter: 2 days
- Min edge: 10-12%
- Use 5-day realized vol (not DVOL)
- Friday seasonality: -1.5% probability penalty (negative avg Friday return)
- FOMC week: skip entirely

## GitHub Repos Found
- quantgalore/kalshi-trading — S&P 500 daily brackets (RF model, closest analog)
- suislanchez/polymarket-kalshi-weather-bot — +1325 USD, ensemble probability, 8% min edge
- CarlosIbCu/polymarket-kalshi-btc-arbitrage-bot — cross-platform arb

## Critical Insight
KXBTCD edge is STRUCTURAL (lognormal mispricing vs options market).
BTCDriftStrategy edge is BEHAVIORAL (momentum). Different instruments entirely.
Do NOT reuse drift strategy for threshold bets.
